case_title: YOU LIANG SONG VS. ARTHUR LIU ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY AUGUST 9, 2022. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-13-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT'S OBLIGATION IS TO CONSTRUE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT "WITH A VIEW TO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE BETWEEN THE PARTIES." (THOMAS V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2023) 97 CAL.APP.5TH 587, 605 [QUOTATION MARKS AND CITATION OMITTED.) WITH THAT STANDARD IN MIND THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR THE COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. PER CCP 436(B), THE COURT STRIKES THE COMPLAINT WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SSEE MINI MINUTES) PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THAT HE IS SUING DEFENDANT FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE, THE PRECISE ACTION/S WHICH SUPPORT THE CLAIM, AND WHEN PLAINTIFF INCURRED INJURY. Motion To Enter Judgment/Request For Entry Of Formal Judgment
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF YOU SONG'S Motion To Enter Judgment/Request For Entry Of Formal Judgment.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff You Song's Motion To Enter Judgment/Request For Entry Of Formal Judgment is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, Defendant Arthur Liu is ordered to prepare, serve and submit a proposed judgment within ten days of notice of this order. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1590(h); see National Secretarial Serv., Inc. v. Froelich (1989) 210 Cal.App.3d 510, 523.) From the date of submission, "Any party may, within 10 days after service of the proposed judgment, serve and file objections thereto." (California Rules of Court, rule 3.1590(j).)

ALL PARTIES are ordered to prepare and submit to Dept. 302 a proposed from of order which quotes verbatim the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff You Liang Song To Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Sanctions, Declaration Of Arthur Liu In Support Of Motion To Compel, Memorandum Of Points And Authorites
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff You Liang Song To Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Sanctions

Defendant Arthur Liu moves to compel responses to his form interrogatories, set one; special interrogatories, set one; and requests for production of documents, set one. The court grants the motion and grants the request for sanctions in part.

Liu served the discovery at issue on July 17, 2025. (Liu Decl., para. 2, & Ex. 1.) Plaintiff You Liang Song served general objections to the special interrogatories on July 19, 2025. (Liu Decl., para. 2, & Ex. 2.) He did not otherwise respond.

Plaintiff's failure to timely respond to the form interrogatories and requests for production waives objections to those discovery requests. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (a); id., section 2031.300, subd. (a).) Those objections asserted in his general objections to the special interrogatories are preserved, but he is still obliged to provide responses.

Plaintiff is ordered to provide complete and straightforward responses to all of the discovery requests within three weeks of notice of entry of this order. He may assert objections only as to the special interrogatories, and only those objections that he preserved with his general objections, but he must answer each special interrogatory or assert an individualized objection to each. He may not rely on general objections to the entire set. Plaintiff's answers must be verified under penalty of perjury.

Liu seeks sanctions. Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in the party's discovery compliance, even where he does not oppose the motion. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2029.290, subd. (c); id., section 2031.300, subd. (c); Rules of Court, Rule 3.1348, subd. (a).) A party who represents himself is required to comply with the Code of Civil Procedure and the Rules of Court. The court does not find substantial justification or unjust circumstances and orders Plaintiff to pay $90 to Liu within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. The court denies Liu's request that Plaintiff pay $1000 to the court but cautions Plaintiff that the court may consider sanctions payable to the court in the future if he does not comply with his discovery obligations. Discovery is not supposed to require court intervention; parties must answer questions and provide documents to each other as provided by the Code of Civil Procedure without the need for a court order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Continued To May-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court's obligation is to construe second amended complaint "with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (Thomas v. Regents of University of California (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 587, 605 [quotation marks and citation omitted.) With that standard in mind the court overrules the demurrer.

The court notes that, in violation of CRC 3.1320(d), Defendant filed the demurrer on October 16, 2024 and set it for hearing nearly seven months later without a showing of good cause. This alone provides a basis to overrule it. Nonetheless the court considers the merits in the alternative. The court denies the request to take judicial notice of numerous prior court files for lack of specificity and for Defendant's failure to comply with Local Rule 8.6(A).

The court construes the document filed by Plaintiff on September 30, 2024 as a second amended complaint. The court construes it as pleading three causes of action: a first cause of action for legal malpractice (appearing in the portion of the document beginning with "Part one:" and continuing through and including the paragraph that discusses contempt of court, and then including the paragraph labeled "abuse of litigation procedures"); a second cause of action for defamation; and a third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, which is how the court construes the paragraph concerning mental distress from being required to translate from Chinese.

Defendant demurs on the grounds of incomprehensibility and untimeliness. The court overrules the first ground; as discussed above the causes of action are sufficiently discernible to provide notice of what is at issue. As to the second ground, the action was filed on December 15, 2021, and at least some of the conduct complained of occurred within any applicable limitations period according to the face of the complaint. Defendant's declaration stating additional facts cannot be considered on demurrer.

Defendant shall respond to the complaint within 10 days as provided in Rule of Court 3.1320(j).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
You Liang Song, 415-799-6055, songyouliang@yahoo.com, Pro Per Plaintiff (In court)
Aidong Ni, Certification # 301135, Court Certified Cantones Interperter (In court)
No other apperaances.

Defendnat, Liu reveiws the Court's tentative ruling with the assitance of Adong Ni, Court Certified Cantonese Interperter.

Hearing is held. Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Per CCP 436(b), the court strikes the complaint with 20 days leave to amend. CCP 425.10(a)(1) provides that the complaint must contain "[a] statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." Plaintiff's complaint does not comply with this rule. The "allegations" refer to extraneous matters and the complaint contains unnecessary exhibits. The pleading is unintelligible. Defendant contends that plaintiff's action is time-barred, but the court cannot discern whether it is given the pleading's incoherence. Plaintiff has leave to amend to clarify that he is suing defendant for legal malpractice, the precise action/s which support the claim, and when plaintiff incurred injury.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
